page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sgn8ia-m2.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine instance configured with dedicated compute resources. It features an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The instance provides 4 physical cores and 4 vCPUs, meaning there is a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio with dedicated CPU allocation. This processing setup is paired with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

For hardware acceleration, the instance includes a single NVIDIA GPU from the Ada Lovelace family, equipped with 2 GB of VRAM. This GPU configuration is integrated directly into the instance, though local storage is not bundled, requiring external block storage volumes for data persistence. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The virtual machine runs on the KVM hypervisor.

Given its hardware profile, the ecs.sgn8ia-m2.xlarge is designed for workloads requiring entry-level GPU acceleration alongside dedicated x86_64 CPU cores. Suitable applications include lightweight machine learning inference, video transcoding, graphics rendering, and general-purpose workloads that benefit from a dedicated CPU-to-RAM ratio of 1:4 combined with a small-capacity GPU.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sgn8ia-m2.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is equipped with an AMD EPYC 9T34 processor running at 3.4 GHz, delivering 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 16.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, and a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Acceleration is provided by a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace GPU with 2 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local storage, requiring network-attached storage for operations. It is designed for workloads that require dedicated CPU resources alongside entry-level GPU acceleration, such as lightweight graphics rendering, video encoding, and small-scale machine learning inference.
